bead.title: Visibility 7: teach execute-loop + execute-bead about --provider and --model-ref, error on orphan --model
bead.description:
The real bug fix from the vidar→OpenRouter silent-fallback incident. Update cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (execute-loop) and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead) to accept three NEW flags alongside the existing --harness and --model:

- --provider <name>: passes through to ddx-agent as its `-provider` flag. Selects a named provider from   config (e.g. `vidar`, `vidar-coder`, `openrouter`).
- --model-ref <ref>: passes through as `-model-ref`. Resolves via the model catalog to a canonical target   (e.g. `code-high`, `code-medium`).
- --model <string>: already exists. Currently passed to ddx-agent as `-model` which does a direct model   override. THE FIX: when --model is given but no matching provider can serve it AND no catalog alias   matches AND no --provider/--model-ref is set, DDx must error BEFORE dispatching the agent — "model 'X'   did not match any provider, catalog ref, or model-route; specify --provider or --model-ref explicitly".   Do NOT silently fall back to the default provider (which was how --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next routed to   OpenRouter earlier).

Wire the new flags through RunOptions / ExecuteBeadOptions / ExecuteBeadLoopOptions so they reach the Runner.RunAgent in internal/agent/agent_runner.go, which then forwards them to agentlib provider resolution.
bead.acceptance: (1) `ddx agent execute-bead <id> --provider vidar` routes to vidar unambiguously. (2) `ddx agent execute-loop --model-ref code-medium` resolves via the model catalog. (3) `ddx agent execute-loop --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next` with NO provider/ref flag errors out with the message described above (regression test required). (4) `ddx agent execute-loop --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next --provider vidar-coder` works correctly and routes to vidar. (5) Unit or integration test covers the orphan-model error case. (6) `go build ./...` passes. (7) `go test ./cli/cmd/... ./cli/internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
